channel.title: q-bio.MN updates on arXiv.org
channel.description: q-bio.MN updates on the arXiv.org e-print archive.

item.title: Control of genes by self-organizing multicellular interaction networks
item.description: arXiv:2603.26530v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Multicellular self-organization drives development in biological organisms, yet a comprehensive theory is lacking as basic properties of cells can complicate common approaches. Framing such properties by dynamic graphs led to new theoretical propositions for multicellular self-organization in Escherichia coli. Here, corresponding ideas are developed from biologically-general first principles. The resulting perspective could aid both experimental and computational approaches to multicellular biology as well as efforts to control and engineer it.
item.dc:creator: Kyle R. Allison
